reviews.review_title: Its is great but started glitching!
reviews.review_description: Please contact me developers, Little Cities is one of my go to games but it became unplayable a few days ago and reinstalling didnt do anything. I recorded what happens so maybe you can find a fix. I really hope so!! Will change the review when its fixed, but this seems to be the only way to reach anyone!
I love playing this game but I hate it when your in sandbox and you only have a limited amount of land pls add unlimited amount of land

I really love this game, it's a good way to expand your imagination and how to build the cities or towns, but one thing that i think that could have been better is if you guys would have add farms, that is one thing that i think would be really good in the game

reviews.review_title: Never seen a game built with actual love
reviews.review_description: This game is one of the very few games out there built with actual love. Cities are cool👍
I have a question 3S and have been wanting tiny town vr but, you need a quest 2 and a pc so I downloaded this game and it is pretty similar to tiny town and I love it but especially the sandbox mode.

I played less then 30 minutes and realised it was a lot different then what I thought which is a valid reason for a refund and it’s saying it is not eligible for a refund when I checked if “little cities is refundable” search says yes! Meta help me
My daughter loves this game but it keeps freezing so she’s not able to play it anymore.

reviews.review_title: Great Game, But There Has To Be More !!
reviews.review_description:
It’s a very well developed and thought out game, with very good mechanics and integration, however it has the potential for much more and in my honest opinion and experience after playing for a while, I believe it would engage and create a much more interesting experience if …
1 - The area you are given to build was made at least twice the size to give you more to do while expanding the landscape for a bigger and better planned out city.
2 - If it had a way to connect different landscapes out of the already set out area that you can build on, while also connecting the different areas to create a even bigger and more immersive ecosystems.
Every time I get a message it stops me from being able to play the game and it won't even let me open it like I just got this game today and I already can't play

reviews.review_title: Good game but kinda a scam
reviews.review_description: The only thing that made it a bit of a scam is the sandbox mode it's good but in a sandbox you most of the time have infinite money no limits no problems just your free mind. Yes the unlocking thing (you have to go earn levels to put more stuff) it's cool but come on you could have added infinite money. Devs pls read this.Over all not bad of a game!

reviews.review_title: Probably the most relaxing game yet
reviews.review_description: It’s always somehow capped at 7000 population wise it would be fun to have bigger maps and bigger building it would look so good

Very good, stress-free city builder. Challenging but not overly complex. Always interesting and surprising. If you've ever played TapTap builder this is like that. I would like to see a space shuttle (if there isn't one that i haven't gotten to yet); a day-night cycle with dynamic shadows; and some interactive capabilities like maybe i could pick someone up, or splash at the boats or whales, or wave at the birds or a plane and make a wind that they respond to.
I have slight trouble hitting some of the controls with my opposing hand accidentally, so it would help at least if the sound effects were more distinctive so at least i can hear what my error was if i didn't see it. And we need a way to identify the buildings that are on the ground like a point & button press, because when I need to demolish something or it burns down i lose track of what may need replacement.
Other than that very good job. One of the few games i play for hours and keep coming back to.
... PS I would also like to see replicas of real-world buildings, landmarks, and attractions...

reviews.review_title: Brilliant!!! but there’s got to be more.
reviews.review_description: Excellent game have so enjoyed playing it so far but…it’s not that addictive in so much as things always look the same, the same things keep happening ie. Aliens, whales etc give us more! Your game has so much potential.
I hope its a good game after seeing all of the reviews it sounds like a good game but I played it on phone and it is really nice 👍
One of the best city sim adaptations for VR. Easy to pick up, and easy on the eyes. Well designed and hand-tracking compatible. Awesome job team!

Hand tracking is some kind of misunderstanding, even with the best lighting. The popup that keeps appearing when selecting an option to buy an add-on for the game keeps popping up during gameplay!!!
A new game started again shows the same tutorial, even in sandbox mode it’s the same.

reviews.review_title: Just needs a little more depth
reviews.review_description:
This is a very slick game. Really nice UI, very intuitive. Graphics are well designed and crisp.
It's definitely pretty relaxing .
I just wish it had a little bit more depth. For example, to allow the zones to mature and develop (simcity style). At the moment it really doesn't matter how the zones go down or how the roads are laid out, everything just works. The only way you can mess it up is if you stick industrial right next to residential, but even then you only need to separate them by a few shops and all is well again.
So my wish list would be for a bit more city management, then it would be perfect.
Love this game relaxing and fun but wish could buy more land i run out and then its done

Hello to the Little Cities team (and Meta Quest reviewers),

I’ve been playing Little Cities for a few months now and really enjoy its relaxing, creative gameplay. But I also find myself wishing there was more to do—especially when it comes to exploring the world I built.

Right now, it feels limited to just zooming and building from above. So I started creating workarounds: I raise my boundary and sit down to pretend I’m a character in the world, but this is tricky and often don’t work.

🧠 The Problem
	•	When my Meta Quest loses the original elevated boundary, I’m forced to make a new one—ruining the illusion I built.
	•	If I sit down and try to move using my hands, the system just zooms me away, often to a rooftop or the middle of the island.
	•	I sometimes accidentally go into build mode, even when I didn’t mean to build anything. That totally breaks the immersion.
	•	You can’t enter shops, homes, or even really interact with objects.
	•	Some areas (like soccer courts) are cute, but static. For example, there’s a goalie—but they always do one pose, and the ball is kicked away automatically, so I can’t actually play.
	•	My avatar hands are these large, blue, see-through shapes, and it doesn’t feel like “me.”
	•	If I try to walk beside someone or along the sidewalk, it rarely works—I just glitch away or teleport backward.
	•	Land and terrain are very basic—just blocky shapes—not as rich or detailed as they could be.

✅ What Would Fix It: A True “Character Mode”

Please consider adding a first-person immersive mode where players can:
	•	Toggle “Character Mode” on/off
	•	Move around naturally (either with joystick or gesture-based walking)
	•	See a basic version of their avatar (hands, body—like in Horizon)
	•	Grab or interact with objects: play soccer, open shop doors, sit on benches, etc.
	•	Walk beside citizens and feel like part of the town
	•	Have the option to start a family, build a home, or play out daily life in the city
	•	More realistic land elevation and detail to make the city feel more alive

Even just the ability to walk around and interact with a few basic things would make Little Cities feel like an entirely new experience.

I believe this would make the game one of the most immersive city-builders in VR—and something people talk about far beyond Meta Quest.

If you’re reading this and agree, please like this post and help bring this feature to life. I’d love for this to be one of the most-liked feature requests on the whole platform so the developers can see how much it matters to us.

Thank you to the team for making such a cozy game already—and for listening to player ideas!
￼

This game is awesome and fun but I would love to see it fleshed out more. Maybe some new DLCs and content. More freedom in the sandbox portion like being able to create bigger islands. I am always disappointed when I run out of land. I could play this game for hours but after awhile I get bored because I've done/used everything the game has to offer.

I enjoy playing this game, and it's very relaxing, minus the occasional volcanic eruption during the campaign levels. I usually play the game in Sandbox Mode with the "Disasters" off so I don't have to worry about volcanic eruptions. To be honest, I was a little confused at first about how to keep my citizens happy, but once I figured out what buildings go where, it was actually pretty easy.

I have always loved city building games! This game makes it fun without the complicated specifics that comes with a city building game. You just build and watch your city grow!! We need more natural disasters, maybe more realistic visuals? Other then that 10/10 for this game!

reviews.review_title: So much potential and sooooo much fun
reviews.review_description: This game is amazing, I feel like a weather cycle would be so cool, and of course, BIGGER BIGGER LAND, this game has so much content and details, and it overall is really fun, looking forward to future updates

So ive just recently gotten my own personal vr account and this was the second game I got, I was utterly lost in it. I recomend this to new vr users if they arent really sure what they should play yet or what games interest them. It was super fun seeing how much detail is actually in the city itself, the ufos were a fun touch!

I picked this game not long after getting my quest 2. Initially I didn’t take to it as I wasn’t sure what to do with it. 

That said, I gave this game another chance and I got utterly lost in it. 

It has Sim City vibes and I love the added touches in the game. Like birds, hot air balloons etc floating past. 

I’m now using the sandbox mode and I love it. It gives you a bit more freedom to play how you like. 

It’s quite peaceful, almost zen like and I love seeing the volcano erupt.

This game just blows me away. I love building games but when you're hovering in the midst of your creations, it's just next level. I love how you can view your cities from high above or drop down to practically street level. The birds and planes passing by are just amazing, and if you zoom in, the details are just impressive. 

I once zoomed in on a guy that was hanging out on a balcony. I watched how the birds opened their wings as they descended in an airstream. 

This type of attention to detail is what makes this game so amazing. The beautiful background music makes for such a comfortable, relaxing experience
This game is amazing it’s really good it makes me feel like screaming ERM WAT DA SIGMA I love it thank you for this game 5/5.
It’s good game it’s not hard to play you don’t need space or need to move but I wish it had better graphics ￼

I was just playing and it got super duper laggy. Couldn’t even get out the game unless I restarted my headset. Went back to it and it never closed the game. Took me right back same spot and lagged again.  Gave up.  Please fix.   Thanks.

I’m doing a good city when realize I got a $1,000,000 money! But can you update a bus pls.
￼

developer_response.body: Thanks for the 5 stars! It definitely sounds like you're enjoying Little Cities and have got the hang of it with $1,000,000. Unfortunately we don't have any further updates planned for Little Cities right now.

reviews.review_description: I like playing this game but sometimes I get nauseous and my neck hurts from looking down.

developer_response.body: Hey HailKingSloth, thanks for the 5 star review! We're so happy to hear you like playing this game. You can change the height of the play area from the main menu using the handle, which will hopefully help with the neck pain, and there are comfort settings in the settings, which may be worth exploring for the nausea :-) We hope this helps you enjoy the game even more.

reviews.review_description: this game is so boring

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear you feel this way about Little Cities.

reviews.review_description: Like with all products one buys, both the item and the customer service matters.  The instructions regarding this game are quite poor.  I ran into issues that were not explained until I went through many messages on their form which went unanswered, followed by having to do a factory restore of my Quest 3.  When that didn't help I finally joined their Discord server and got answers.  My rating is based on their lack of good customer service and their total lack of empathy.  Also they denied getting my messages which is hard to believe given I sent many messages. Overall a major disappointment.  The app itself can be fun once one gets through the hurdles caused by poor instructions.

developer_response.body: Hi elash99, we're sorry our customer service didn't live up to your expectations this time, but we're very happy that the issue is now resolved. We hope you continue to enjoy the Little Cities Experience!

reviews.review_description:
I play this a lot when I want a stress free relaxing game. It’s really fun to watch your city population grow and your people enjoying the places you've built for them. 
I have played several hours in this game as it continues to be a steady goto even after owning it for a few years now.

developer_response.body: Hi LatissaKatrice, thank you so much for your 5 star review! We love hearing how relaxing you find Little Cities and hope you continue to enjoy building your dream city :-)

reviews.review_description:
First off I have to preface that I bought this game close to launch, played it 5 minutes, then wrote it off for being significantly simpler than the other city builder that came out around the same time (Cities VR). Since launch, this game has received multiple content and quality of life improvements.

I tried it again recently and was immediately impressed with how intuitive the game handles controls compared to Cities VR. Everything is straightforward and explained well to the player so I never felt lost or unable to find a building I need. The menu on your wrist tells you everything you need to know as far as what needs to be taken care of to keep everyone happy. Sometimes I completely ignore it and just build however I want. I've completed about 5 or 6 islands now and plan to buy the snow Island DLC. 

The calming music sets a great vibe that cannot be understated - I wish more games had music this good. I hummed along to the tunes especially in the desert Islands. While cities VR could be a very stressful experience, the tone of Little Cities is very much about being creative, appreciating the little touches, slowing down and just enjoying building this small world for your little people. I actually wish you could get even lower to the ground with your camera so you could really see what it looks like from the street with all the people walking around and living life. At launch there were no citizens walking around so if feels much more alive now. I especially appreciate the things flying like hot air balloons, airplanes, and even flying saucers that wizz by your face. It all feels very immersive and appropriate for this genre.

Overall, if you want a chill city builder with low stakes that you can vibe to and have fun, I 100% recommend this game. I especially love the support from the devs with updates, something Cities VR has lacked and left me disappointed in.

developer_response.body: Hey Ralkion, thanks for the five-star review and for giving Little Cities another chance! We always wanted it to be a more accessible and relaxing experience, which is what comes across in your thoughts. We're especially glad you like the music, it's so easy to get lost in it!

reviews.review_description: SimCity 2000 has more functionality and strategy than this game.  I'm only an hour in and I'll update this if I am wrong but jeesh... There isn't much to the game, just 3 zones types and not much variety in what pops up for buildings.  It has a lot of downtime waiting and there isn't enough stuff to look at to stay interesting. It feels like a demo.
Can't review the actual game because the  game won't even download

developer_response.body: So sorry to hear that you're experiencing this issue. This isn't an issue that we've seen before and we're keen to get this fixed for you as soon as possible. Could you send some details on the issue to our support email inbox - support@ndreams.com

reviews.review_description:
⚡It's the best game on quest! ⚡
Get it 25%off the price below
q u e s t g a m e . b i o . l i n k

Fun game and all but after a day of playing it just doesn’t work. I open the app and it makes me choose a save file over and over again. It’s seemed like a problem with my WiFi or something, so I unplugged my router. Nothing. So I tried switch WiFi networks. Nothing. I tried uninstalling the game. Nothing. It just won’t work. It might be some sort of problem on my end but I hope there is a way to fix it.

developer_response.body: We really appreciate you bringing this to our attention. This is the first report we've had of any issue like this. Please contact us via support@ndreams.com so we can troubleshoot and investigate this for you. If you have video footage of this problem occurring that would be endlessly helpful.

reviews.review_description: It sucks and you can’t get a refund

developer_response.body: Incredibly disappointing to read you didn't enjoy your time with Little Cities. We always welcome constructive feedback from our community. If you feel you should still be eligible for a refund, you can find the Quest content refund policy here: https://www.meta.com/gb/en/legal/quest/quest-rift-content-refund-policy/

reviews.review_description: The game is fun for about 15 minutes but after that it is a boring game,some free games are much better and dont get me started on how expensive the game is for what you get

developer_response.body:
We're incredibly disappointed to read that you didn't enjoy your experience with Little Cities. We're confident that at the price point it's at, having enjoyed a year of free updates, that Little Cities offers lots of opportunities for fun and great value for money, but appreciate that this game just wasn't for you. 

In June, we're launching the Sandbox Update, which will allow for endless creativity, with players able to create their own islands from scratch by manipulating the terrain of each map. We'd love to welcome you back and get your thoughts on the game once this next free update launches.

reviews.review_description: Basically do the tutorial and then you're done. After I did the tutorial and there was nothing else to do I spent the next two weeks trying to find a way to get a refund and just kept getting the runaround and never got a refund.  I really wish they would add a zero star selection because that's what it deserves

developer_response.body:
We're really disappointed to hear you did not enjoy your experience with Little Cities as there's lots to uncover and create beyond the tutorial, with a variety of different islands, each with their own challenges, and lots of freedom for player expression and discovery. 

Refunds are handled by the platform holder, Meta in this case, and you can find their policy here: https://www.meta.com/gb/legal/quest/quest-rift-content-refund-policy/

reviews.review_description: Sim City this is not.  I wanted to like it.  It started out like sim City, building houses, industry or commercial.  Then I got a fire station and a police station.  I got up to about 1400 residents.  Then...nothing.  The city just sat there.  Looking at my watch showed no progress or decline.  My town was completely built up.  I just sat there waiting for some kind of indicator or something to tell me what I should do next.  In sim City you could always look at your city and see what was needed.  This game shows very little of that info.  So after watching paint dry for about a half hour, I was bored out of my skull and left the game, never to return.

developer_response.body:
We're of course disappointed to read that you did not enjoy your time with Little Cities. Once your population reaches 1400 during the tutorial, you should receive a notification on your wristwatch that you've unlocked emergency services (such as fire rescue and police), as well as cell towers and the medical centre. 

Placing these will allow your city to keep expanding, with residents now having access to appropriate needs. From there, you'd check the wristwatch and determine what buildings from the pie chart are required most from residential, commercial, to industrial - growing your population until you reach 1500 (when the next region of the map unlocks for you to expand into). 

If your progress seems to have stalled, and this isn't the case, we'd love to investigate the issue with you. Please reach out to support@ndreams.com.

reviews.review_description: I have had this game for 4 days and I have already finished 2 maps starting a 3rd. This game is so satisfying to play and Calms me down. There is some minor bugs in hand tracking mode but overall great game

reviews.review_title: Got through the tutorial but then.
reviews.review_description: It seems my build menu dissappear under the water or land depending where I look and is stuck there.  I have no option but to quit since I start  in the same place again if I restart the app. Will update score when I can get further into the game. Can't do a thing but watch seagulls, boats, and airplanes float and fly around the island.

developer_response.body: Hello, Thank for bringing this to our attention and we are sorry to hear about this unusual experience you've had in Little Cities. Please email support@ndreams.com with details of the issue, steps to reproduce, and the equipment being used, so that we can investigate this further. Thanks.

reviews.review_description: Just paid $20 for this game and it won’t install

developer_response.body: Hello, We are sorry to hear that the game will not install on your device. To help us investigate this further please email support@ndreams.com with details (such as device used, steps to reproduce and any error messages) so that we can provide assistance.

reviews.review_description: The cost is way to high for what you get.

developer_response.body: Thank you for leaving this review and we are sorry to hear that you found Little Cities to be disappointing. We’d love to hear more of your experience and what you’d like to see included in the game, so please do reach out to us via support@ndreams.com. In the meantime we are working on post-launch content, such as hand tracking and cosmetic items that bring more ways to create your city, so these will also be worth checking out in the coming months.

reviews.review_title: Factory reset lost all my cities.
reviews.review_description: I lost my city progress.

developer_response.body: Hi there, we are sorry to hear that your save data has been lost after doing a factory reset, but you may be able to retrieve a backup of this save data from the cloud. Save data in Little Cities is automatically backed up via Oculus Cloud Sync (if enabled) and will redownload once the game has been reinstalled on your device. If you are experiencing issues with Oculus Cloud Sync, or are unable to retrieve stored data online, then we would suggest reaching out to Meta Support directly via https://store.facebook.com/en-gb/help/support/quest/topic/software to see if they can help further. Hopefully this will help restore your lost data so that you can get back to creating in Little Cities. Should you require any additional support with Little Cities then please do reach out to us via support@ndreams.com

reviews.review_description: It’s a very fun city builder that you can get lost in.
It was very high quality and I had fun on it for hours
Probably my new favorite game! Very relaxing and all the small details of activities in the city is awesome
Overall a very good game. It can make you mad at some points. Like,there are some points where it’s very hard to get your population up so you can’t level up your city. But it is a pretty fun game.
Sim City in a whole new way! Need more land though. Quests and/or natural disasters would be a good challenge!

developer_response.body: Thank you for leaving a review and for sharing your feedback of Little Cities with us. Little Cities does features multiple islands with each having different challenges to overcome, such as the Desert Island with sandstorms and the Volcano island with volcanic eruptions, and these islands will become accessible as you progress through the game. To access a new island once unlocked simply go to the build menu and tap on the world map bubble to be taken to the island selection table. We also have more content planned for Little Cities in the future, including hand tracking and cosmetic items.

reviews.review_description:
Do like the game, but needs more challenge. Like another player said, once you've maxed out, that's it! Pretty much have done so with light play time in a month. Like, islands have volcanoes, let the islands expand with each eruption.  Or incorporate emergency management skills by including tsunamis, flooding, hurricanes, mudslides or island defense from invaders. Could add bigger islands to additional levels of play. Make terrain of additional islands challenging by adding hills, mountains, lakes, etc.

Like I said, really do enjoy the game, but developers need to expand on it. I'd pay a couple bucks for add ons.

developer_response.body: We are glad to hear that you enjoyed the game and thank you for the feedback relating to island layouts and natural disasters. In the meantime we do have plans for post launch content, with hand tracking now available in the game and cosmetic items in the near future.

reviews.review_description: I like this game but would love to see little people walking around, day\night cycle and more islands as it's a little short. Other than that good game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the kind words and we are glad to hear your enjoying Little Cities. We also appreciate these suggestions, so we'll be sure to pass them along, but in the meantime we've just released a new update for Little Cities that adds over 30 new attractions and a style rating system that offers creative challenges and new ways to play.

EDIT: We're happy to finally confirm the start of our post-launch roadmap for 2022/23. The next update, launching in December, will feature two brand new islands and lots of snowy shenanigans, followed closely in early 2023 by "itty bitty citizenry" that will add little residents and more life to your creations!

EDIT (March 2023): We're so excited to share that the Little Citizens update is available for Little Cities right now! Welcome this tiny population to your creations as they dynamically interact with the cities you build.

reviews.review_title: This game is way to short
reviews.review_description: It’s a cute game but way to short only 6 islands to build on I beat the game in 2 days pretty sad probably never get on it again unless new islands get added would also like to see a multiplayer to the game with a massive world and you gotta buy chunks of land also would be cool to like drive around or walk around the city’s but other wise waste of money I’ll never recommend it to any one

developer_response.body: Thank you for leaving a review and for sharing your feedback with us. We are also sorry to hear that you found the game to be a bit too short, but we do have plans to add additional content in the near future.

reviews.review_description:
It's a great game to casually play I enjoyed it. I was on my 3rd island about halfway through. Next time I went to play I started on island 1 with nothing. Rebooted headset, nothing changed. For a game that is built around designing and creating unique things, when you lose that time and effort that's a big deal I can only give 2 stars. If losing my progress never happened it would be a 4 or 5 star. How can I trust that if I start again my work won't be deleted again? Sucks cause I paid money for this and may never play again.
UPDATE JULY 4: Thanks to the developer response I found my data/progress. It ended up the game was in a different save slot. I feel stupid actually. But in my defense I don't know how that happened, the game had always been in the same save slot. I'll keep an eye on it.

developer_response.body:
We are sorry to hear about your experience with Little Cities and it is the first time we have heard of save data being lost after closing the game. We'd like to hear more about this issue, so please do email support@ndreams.com with details so that we can investigate further.

In the meantime Little Cities does feature six save slots, which can be accessed from your Smart Watch while playing the game, so we'd suggest checking here to see if the game is using a different save slot as when a new save slot is selected it will automatically load that save slot.

reviews.review_description: I put only 4 starts because this game is too cartoony. But it’s so fun and relaxing. You don’t have to stress out about gameplay. Super easy to build!

developer_response.body: Thank you for leaving a review and we are glad to hear you found Little Cities fun and relaxing to play.

reviews.review_description: Exactly what i wanted, easy controls to pick up, and not too much to be confusing. However i do wish there was some type of multiplayer, view other peoples cities and such, maybe a way to connect your islands together
